---
bibliography: references.bib
---

# 3. Scenario

We are interested in exploring the implications of allowing AI, a service generated by the information industry, to replace labor at various costs, for different industries and for different regions. Specifically, we focus on clerical labor which is the GTAP labor type that is most likely to be replaced by AI in all industries.[^1]

[^1]: @manning2026 note that clerical and administrative roles are most at risk. They are both considered clerical in GTAP.

We implement this scenario with several modalities. First, we consider two degrees of global adoption of the AI labor replacement technology: (1) an adoption by the developed countries only and (2) a global adoption. The second modality relates to the supply of clerical labor during the proces of adopting AI to replace it; for each scenario we then assume that (1) no policy action will be taken and that (2) a 100 percent tax on AI use in replacing clerical labor is adopted.

In all of the scenarios we assume that nominal wages remains fixed and, instead, clerical labor will exit/enter the labor force to maintian this wage. Note that in the standard GTAP model, full employment is assumed (and wages adjust)–which corresponds to our first assumption. To implement the second assumption, we swap the two in the closure statement such that employment adjusts while wages are fixed.

To see the implications of taxation on AI's benefits, we also produce scenario where we allow AI to be adopted everywhere and where we apply a range of use taxes ranging from 0 to 100 percent. 

```{=html}
<!-- ## Replacing professional staff with AI

In the second set of scenarios, we consider the replacement of technical staff with AI in the manufacturing and service sector. Again, we implement the adotpion of AI in three stages, first by each country alon, then by all developed countries and then, finally, by the entire world. We, again, test two different costs for AI implementation as in the scenarios mentioned above.

## Putting it all together

In the final set of scenarios we analyze the situation where both clerical labor in all industries and technical staff in manufacturing and services is replaced by AI at different costs by developed countries and the entire world.



 -->
```
